title: Cinderella
lines: A man had a beloved wife who fell ill and died.
Then he took another wife, but she had a hard, cruel heart.
She was very unkind to her stepdaughter, the man's beautiful daughter from his first marriage.
The girl's name was Cinderella, and her stepmother made her do all the hard work in the house.
After her father died, things became even worse for poor Cinderella.
She had to sleep by the fire among the ashes, and everyone called her Cinderella because she was always covered in cinders.
One day the king announced a great ball to be given at the palace, where the prince would choose a bride.
All the ladies in the kingdom were invited.
The stepmother and her two daughters made great preparations for the ball.
But Cinderella was not allowed to go.
When the others had left for the ball, Cinderella sat weeping by the fire.
Suddenly a bright light appeared, and her godmother, who was a fairy, stood before her.
"You shall go to the ball," said the fairy.
With a touch of her wand, Cinderella's rags were changed to a beautiful gown.
Her old shoes became glass slippers, and a splendid coach drawn by six white horses appeared.
"But you must leave before midnight," warned the fairy, "for at the stroke of twelve the spell will be broken."
At the ball, the prince was so enchanted by Cinderella's beauty that he danced with her all evening.
But as midnight approached, Cinderella remembered the fairy's warning and fled.
In her haste, she lost one of her glass slippers on the palace steps.
The next day the prince sent his servants throughout the kingdom with the glass slipper.
Every lady tried it on, but it fit no one.
When the servants came to Cinderella's house, her stepsisters tried in vain to squeeze their feet into the slipper.
Then Cinderella asked if she might try.
The slipper fit her perfectly.
The prince was brought to the house and recognized his beautiful partner from the ball.
He took Cinderella to the palace, and they were married.
Cinderella's goodness and patience had been rewarded, and she lived happily ever after.
